biography_with_entities.raw_text:
What I feel, I vomit on paper. 🌶 ❤️🎵🤡
> #Escribujos en posters by @fiogiuseppe
-
POSTERS DM
#drawritings

node.text: Pain is inevitable, suffering is optional #visceralpoems

Sometimes, the only way I can breathe is by writing and drawing at the same time.

Three years ago, during a long journey through Latin America, I started carrying a small notebook with me. In it, I poured out words that weren’t just words they twisted, danced, and transformed into shapes. They became escribujos: poems that look like drawings, drawings that feel like poems.

That notebook saved me. It gave me a place to let my emotions bleed, to turn pain into tenderness, and to remind myself that even the most raw feelings can be shared with kindness.

What began in those intimate pages slowly escaped: onto posters on strangers’ walls, onto the streets, into the hands and homes of people I’ve never met. From my heart to theirs. From my silence to the noise of the world.

Visceral Poems is not just my project it’s my way of saying: I see you, I feel you, I’m with you.
For my brothers and sisters out there who sometimes forget they are not alone.

This is my honest way to vomit love. To turn fragility into strength. To make you cry, and maybe also smile. ❤️🌑🪐🪶🥹🌊💥🔥

Prints or handmade available ❤️

#visceralpoems 

Follow the page here @visceralpoems
